selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 28.1% and 20-day momentum is 43.2%.
Trend quality is supported by 70% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 25.9% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 3.8%, downside volatility near 2.4%, and a recent max drawdown of 7.4%.
Volume confirmation is -8.7% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 26.0% versus SPY over 5 days and 18.2% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.83, confidence 0.78, and 0.4 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.83, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.4 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.56, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.78.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.230 with high confidence.

news_evidence.title: Texas emerges as US' new economic powerhouse amid California exodus
news_evidence.llm_reason: General report about Texas attracting companies. Oracle has a significant Austin presence (headquarters move in prior years), so the trend is tangentially supportive long-term, but the article contains no Oracle-specific news and is unlikely to move ORCL in the next 1–10 trading days.

news_evidence.title: NVIDIA Unveils Vera, the CPU for Agents
news_evidence.llm_reason: NVIDIA unveiling a new CPU affects the broader AI/data-center hardware ecosystem, which is indirectly relevant to cloud providers including Oracle. However, it is not Oracle-specific and does not present a clear short-term catalyst for ORCL; impact is modest and ambiguous (could expand demand for cloud infrastructure but also benefits competitors).
